name: Wright-Patt Credit Union
description_short: Water and Power Community Credit Union (WPCCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, and savings products. Celebrating 90 years of service with competitive rates and community focus.
description_long:
Water and Power Community Credit Union (WPCCU) has been serving its membership for 90 years, positioning itself as a member-first financial institution. The credit union operates as a not-for-profit organization owned by its members, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. WPCCU is federally insured through the NCUA, providing deposit protection for member accounts.

WPCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including auto loans (APR as low as 4.74%), mortgages and home equity lines of credit (HELOC at 7.75% APR), credit cards, certificates of deposit (APY up to 3.90%), IRAs, and various loan products including their "Power Cash Loan." Members can also access retirement planning services through LPL Financial advisors and participate in financial webinars. The credit union emphasizes digital convenience with online and mobile banking, eStatements, and the ability to make loan payments from other financial institutions without logging into WPCCU's banking platform.

WPCU distinguishes itself through community engagement and member benefits. The organization actively supports local veterans through community outreach and sponsorships, and maintains active fraud prevention protocols with a dedicated fraud reporting system. The credit union recently promoted a special 90th Anniversary Savings Certificate (3.90% APY, 15-month term, $1,000 minimum) and offers loan payment alerts. Their emphasis on financial education through webinars and personalized financial planning demonstrates a commitment to member financial literacy.

As a credit union, WPCCU's primary advantage is its not-for-profit structure, meaning profits return to members through better rates and lower fees rather than to shareholders. However, specific membership eligibility requirements are not detailed on the visible website content, and comparative rate competitiveness with other credit unions cannot be fully assessed from available information. The organization appears well-established and stable with active community involvement and modern digital banking infrastructure.
address: 1761 W Lane Ave, Upper Arlington, OH 43221

pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members through competitive rates
NCUA federal insurance protects member deposits
Competitive auto loan rates (as low as 4.74% APR) and HELOC rates (7.75% APR)
Access to LPL Financial advisors for retirement planning and investment services
Robust fraud protection with dedicated reporting system and fraud alerts
Digital convenience including loan payments from external financial institutions
Active community engagement including local veteran support programs
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly disclosed on website, potentially limiting access
Limited rate comparison data available; cannot confirm competitiveness versus other credit unions
Service geographic scope and branch locations not specified in provided website content
Retirement services provided through third-party LPL Financial rather than in-house advisory
No information on whether membership is open to all or restricted to specific employee groups/employers
best_for: Members seeking member-owned financial institution with community focus and veteran support
Borrowers looking for competitive auto loans and HELOC rates with digital banking convenience
Savers interested in certificates of deposit and retirement planning with advisory support
diagnosis: WPCCU is best for individuals who are members of this credit union and seeking competitive consumer lending rates combined with member-owned, not-for-profit banking. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not publicly detailed on their website, suggesting eligibility may be restricted to specific groups or employers rather than open to all consumers seeking a credit union.
services: Home equity lines of credit - HELOC (7.75% APR)
Retirement planning services via LPL Financial
Loan payment services accessible from external financial institutions
